headline: Penang Second Bridge Maintenance Costs Triple Original Estimates
context: The Sultan Abdul Halim Muadzam Shah Bridge connecting Penang mainland and island reported maintenance costs tripling to RM280 million annually by 2025, from the RM95 million projected during the RM4.5 billion construction phase.

cards.big: RM280 million yearly to maintain one bridge — triple the original estimate — a recurring cost taxpayers never agreed to
cards.sub: 24km bridge's maintenance costs RM11.7 million per kilometre annually — highest infrastructure maintenance cost in Malaysia

cards.big: Cumulative maintenance spending of RM1.4 billion over 10 years approaches 31% of original RM4.5 billion construction cost
cards.sub: Lifecycle cost analysis showing RM280 million annual maintenance was never presented to parliament before construction approval

cards.big: Bridge toll revenue of RM180 million covers only 64% of maintenance cost — federal subsidy fills the RM100 million annual gap
cards.sub: Penang residents argue they shouldn't pay tolls on a bridge that costs more to maintain than it generates in revenue

cards.big: Maintenance contract awarded to same consortium that built the bridge — no competitive tender for RM280 million annual spending
cards.sub: JKR signed 20-year maintenance agreement in 2014 with only 3-yearly review — locked into above-market rates

cards.big: A bridge that costs RM280 million a year to maintain while generating only RM180 million in tolls is a permanent fiscal drain — not infrastructure
Should all mega-project approvals require mandatory 30-year lifecycle cost analysis and competitive maintenance tendering?
